title: Transaction Control on Microsoft SQL Server
breadcrumbs: Referential Integrity Management on Microsoft SQL Server
Transaction Control on Microsoft SQL Server
text: Transaction Control on Microsoft SQL Server Commit and Rollback Both the commit and rollback instructions are fully supported by Microsoft SQL Server. Two-phase commit is not supported. Starting Point of a Transaction Transactions are started by the ODBC connector manager when required. Implicit Rollbacks If an update or a delete causes a trigger to rollback the transaction, no work is allowed on the logon path until the kernel issues a request to end the transaction. This prevents users from performing work that they believe is inside the transaction that has been rolled back. Note: Users should not modify the MS-SQL triggers created by Uniface. Related Links Transaction Control A transaction is a complete, logical unit of work. It usually involves adding, deleting or updating one or more logically related records in one or more databases. With Uniface, you can explicitly define a transaction with the commit ProcScript instruction. If an error occurs, a transaction can be rolled back with the rollback ProcScript instruction.
